attachment.content:
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- rbk-wrangle: Muster, then roster RBK and JJK heats as two space-padded plain-text blocks
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/rbgjl/rbgjl02-assemble-push-vouch.sh
/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/rbgjl/rbgjl01-ensconce-capture.sh

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# RBGJL Step 02: Push each Lode's provenance envelope as its :rbi_vouch artifact
3	# Builder: docker (from reliquary)
4	# Entrypoint: bash
5	# Substitutions: _RBGL_GAR_HOST, _RBGL_GAR_PATH, _RBGL_LODES_ROOT, _RBGL_TAG_VOUCH
6	#
7	# Note: The Dockerfile echo-sequence below is intentional — this script runs
8	# inside a Cloud Build container, not under BCG module discipline.
9	#
10	# Step 01 (skopeo) staged one envelope per captured Lode at
11	# /workspace/lode_<stamp>_vouch.json and listed the stamps in
12	# /workspace/lode_stamps.txt. Each envelope rides into the SAME package as the
13	# base manifest, under the :rbi_vouch tag — a distinct manifest, one per Lode.
14	# Vouch content is architecture-independent; single-platform is sufficient.
15	
16	set -euo pipefail
17	
18	echo "=== Assemble and push Lode vouch artifacts ==="
19	
20	test -f /workspace/lode_stamps.txt \
21	  || { echo "FATAL: /workspace/lode_stamps.txt not found — step 01 must run first" >&2; exit 1; }
22	test -s /workspace/lode_stamps.txt \
23	  || { echo "FATAL: /workspace/lode_stamps.txt is empty — nothing ensconced" >&2; exit 1; }
24	
25	test -n "${_RBGL_TAG_VOUCH}" || { echo "FATAL: _RBGL_TAG_VOUCH missing" >&2; exit 1; }
26	
27	# Ensure the shared buildx builder — shared library snippet (run once).
28	#@rbgjs_include buildx-bootstrap
29	
30	while IFS= read -r STAMP || test -n "${STAMP}"; do
31	  test -n "${STAMP}" || continue
32	
33	  ENVELOPE_FILE="/workspace/lode_${STAMP}_vouch.json"
34	  test -f "${ENVELOPE_FILE}" \
35	    || { echo "FATAL: envelope not staged for ${STAMP}: ${ENVELOPE_FILE}" >&2; exit 1; }
36	
37	  VOUCH_URI="${_RBGL_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGL_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP}:${_RBGL_TAG_VOUCH}"
38	  echo "--- Vouch for ${STAMP} -> ${VOUCH_URI} ---"
39	
40	  CTX="/workspace/vouch_ctx_${STAMP}"
41	  mkdir -p "${CTX}"
42	  cp "${ENVELOPE_FILE}" "${CTX}/vouch.json"
43	  echo "FROM scratch"        >  "${CTX}/Dockerfile"
44	  echo "COPY vouch.json /"   >> "${CTX}/Dockerfile"
45	
46	  # Push the FROM-scratch vouch context — shared library snippet.
47	  PUSH_URI="${VOUCH_URI}"
48	  PUSH_PLATFORMS="linux/amd64"
49	  PUSH_CTX="${CTX}"
50	#@rbgjs_include buildx-push
51	
52	  echo "Vouch pushed: ${VOUCH_URI}"
53	done < /workspace/lode_stamps.txt
54	
55	echo "=== Vouch push step complete ==="
56

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# RBGJL Step 01: Ensconce a base image into a Lode (capture) via skopeo
3	# Builder: skopeo (from reliquary)
4	# Substitutions: _RBGL_GAR_HOST, _RBGL_GAR_PATH, _RBGL_LODES_ROOT,
5	#                _RBGL_TAG_BOLE, _RBGL_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX,
6	#                _RBGL_TRUST_GRADE, _RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA, _RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY,
7	#                _RBGL_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN, _RBGL_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN, _RBGL_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN,
8	#                _RBGL_LODE_1_STAMP,   _RBGL_LODE_2_STAMP,   _RBGL_LODE_3_STAMP
9	#
10	# For each non-empty (ORIGIN, STAMP) slot: inspect upstream, measure the canonical
11	# digest, copy --all into ONE GAR package rbi_ld/<stamp>, then apply the member
12	# tags by GAR->GAR retag (dedups blobs). Author the provenance envelope and stage
13	# it for step 02 (the :rbi_vouch artifact) and for the host capture-file via
14	# /builder/outputs/output. Mason SA ambient auth via Cloud Build metadata server.
15	#
16	# Package shape:  <host>/<path>/<LODES_ROOT>/<stamp>            (one package = one Lode)
17	# Member tags on that package, all pointing at the base manifest:
18	#   :<TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX><full-hex>   canonical OCI digest (exact cross-Lode dedup)
19	#   :<TAG_BOLE>                      uniform greppable handle
20	#   :<sanitized-origin>-<sha10>      UNSPRUED — name + glance-fingerprint (= legacy enshrine anchor)
21	# The :rbi_vouch tag is a separate manifest pushed by step 02.
22	
23	set -euo pipefail
24	echo "=== Ensconce base images into Lodes ==="
25	
26	# Obtain OAuth2 token from metadata server (Mason SA) — shared library snippet.
27	#@rbgjs_include token-fetch
28	
29	# CB substitutions are expanded at submit time, not available as shell variables.
30	# Capture each into a runtime variable so we can loop.
31	SLOT_1_ORIGIN="${_RBGL_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN}"
32	SLOT_2_ORIGIN="${_RBGL_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN}"
33	SLOT_3_ORIGIN="${_RBGL_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN}"
34	SLOT_1_STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_1_STAMP}"
35	SLOT_2_STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_2_STAMP}"
36	SLOT_3_STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_3_STAMP}"
37	
38	# Acquisition moment, attested once for the whole build.
39	ACQUIRED_AT=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
40	
41	# Stamp roster for step 02 (one line per captured Lode).
42	: > /workspace/lode_stamps.txt
43	
44	# Initialize result JSON (host reads this back as the capture-file source)
45	RESULT='{'
46	FIRST=true
47	
48	for SLOT in 1 2 3; do
49	  case "${SLOT}" in
50	    1) ORIGIN="${SLOT_1_ORIGIN}"; STAMP="${SLOT_1_STAMP}" ;;
51	    2) ORIGIN="${SLOT_2_ORIGIN}"; STAMP="${SLOT_2_STAMP}" ;;
52	    3) ORIGIN="${SLOT_3_ORIGIN}"; STAMP="${SLOT_3_STAMP}" ;;
53	  esac
54	  test -n "${ORIGIN}" || continue
55	  test -n "${STAMP}"  || { echo "FATAL: slot ${SLOT} has ORIGIN but no STAMP" >&2; exit 1; }
56	
57	  echo "--- Slot ${SLOT}: ${ORIGIN} -> ${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP} ---"
58	
59	  # Inspect upstream, take the canonical digest, derive the glance fingerprint —
60	  # shared library snippet (requires ORIGIN + RAW_FILE; provides SHA + FINGERPRINT).
61	  RAW_FILE="/workspace/ensconce_raw_${SLOT}.json"
62	#@rbgjs_include skopeo-fingerprint
63	
64	  DIGEST_TAG="${_RBGL_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX}${SHA}"
65	  PKG="${_RBGL_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGL_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP}"
66	
67	  echo "Package: ${PKG}"
68	  echo "Digest:  sha256:${SHA}"
69	  echo "Tags:    ${DIGEST_TAG}, ${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}, ${FINGERPRINT}"
70	
71	  # Collision guard — never silently clobber an existing Lode. The bole handle
72	  # (:rbi_bole) always points at this touchmark's captured image, so inspect it:
73	  # absent => a fresh touchmark; present => re-use. Re-use is legitimate ONLY when
74	  # it is the identical canonical digest (a Cloud Build retry re-copying the same
75	  # bytes); a different digest under the same touchmark is a clobber and fails loud.
76	  # The check sits immediately before the copy in this same step — atomic with the
77	  # GAR write in the sense the spec requires (no host pre-submit window). EXISTING_SHA
78	  # is computed the same way as SHA (sha256sum of the raw manifest), so the compare is
79	  # apples-to-apples. An inspect failure is treated as absent: cloud-side auth/network
80	  # is reliable, and a genuine GAR outage fails the copy below regardless.
81	  EXISTING_RAW="/workspace/ensconce_existing_${SLOT}.json"
82	  if skopeo inspect --raw "docker://${PKG}:${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}" \
83	       --creds "oauth2accesstoken:${TOKEN}" > "${EXISTING_RAW}" 2>/dev/null; then
84	    EXISTING_SHA=$(sha256sum "${EXISTING_RAW}" | cut -d' ' -f1)
85	    if [ "${EXISTING_SHA}" = "${SHA}" ]; then
86	      echo "Touchmark ${STAMP} already holds identical digest sha256:${SHA} — idempotent retry, proceeding."
87	    else
88	      echo "FATAL: touchmark collision at ${PKG}" >&2
89	      echo "  existing :${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE} -> sha256:${EXISTING_SHA}" >&2
90	      echo "  refusing to clobber with  -> sha256:${SHA}" >&2
91	      echo "  (banish the Lode first, or ensconce under a fresh touchmark)" >&2
92	      exit 1
93	    fi
94	  else
95	    echo "Touchmark ${STAMP} is fresh (no existing :${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE})."
96	  fi
97	
98	  # Copy upstream into the Lode package under the canonical digest tag.
99	  skopeo copy --all \
100	    "docker://${ORIGIN}" \
101	    "docker://${PKG}:${DIGEST_TAG}" \
102	    --dest-creds "oauth2accesstoken:${TOKEN}" \
103	    || { echo "FATAL: skopeo copy failed for slot ${SLOT}" >&2; exit 1; }
104	
105	  # Apply remaining member tags by GAR->GAR retag (same blobs, manifest re-tag only).
106	  for MEMBER_TAG in "${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}" "${FINGERPRINT}"; do
107	    skopeo copy --all \
108	      "docker://${PKG}:${DIGEST_TAG}" \
109	      "docker://${PKG}:${MEMBER_TAG}" \
110	      --src-creds  "oauth2accesstoken:${TOKEN}" \
111	      --dest-creds "oauth2accesstoken:${TOKEN}" \
112	      || { echo "FATAL: skopeo retag ${MEMBER_TAG} failed for slot ${SLOT}" >&2; exit 1; }
113	  done
114	
115	  echo "Slot ${SLOT} ensconced: ${STAMP}"
116	
117	  # Author the provenance envelope (identical content lands in :rbi_vouch and the
118	  # host capture-file). No jq dependency — values are controlled (sanitized origin,
119	  # hex digest, SA email, build id, ISO timestamp); none can carry a literal quote.
120	  # members[] is the cardinality axis — length 1 for the bole singleton.
121	  ENVELOPE='{'
122	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"schema\":\"${_RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA}\","
123	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"kind\":\"bole\","
124	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"lode\":\"${STAMP}\","
125	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"acquired_at\":\"${ACQUIRED_AT}\","
126	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"acquired_by\":\"${_RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY}\","
127	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"capture_build\":\"${BUILD_ID:-}\","
128	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"trust_grade\":\"${_RBGL_TRUST_GRADE}\","
129	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"signature\":null,"
130	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"members\":[{"
131	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"name\":\"${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}\","
132	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"origin\":\"${ORIGIN}\","
133	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"digest\":\"sha256:${SHA}\","
134	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"verification\":\"oci-digest\","
135	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"tags\":[\"${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}\",\"${DIGEST_TAG}\",\"${FINGERPRINT}\"]"
136	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}}]}"
137	
138	  # Stage the envelope for step 02 (pushes it as the :rbi_vouch artifact).
139	  printf '%s' "${ENVELOPE}" > "/workspace/lode_${STAMP}_vouch.json"
140	  echo "${STAMP}" >> /workspace/lode_stamps.txt
141	
142	  # Accumulate host-facing result (the capture-file carries the same envelope).
143	  if [ "${FIRST}" = "true" ]; then
144	    FIRST=false
145	  else
146	    RESULT="${RESULT},"
147	  fi
148	  RESULT="${RESULT}\"slot_${SLOT}\":{\"stamp\":\"${STAMP}\",\"vouch\":${ENVELOPE}}"
149	done
150	
151	RESULT="${RESULT}}"
152	
153	echo "=== Writing capture results ==="
154	echo "${RESULT}"
155	
156	# Write to buildStepOutputs channel (host extracts per-Lode envelope -> capture-file)
157	mkdir -p /builder/outputs
158	printf '%s' "${RESULT}" > /builder/outputs/output
159	
160	echo "=== Ensconce capture step complete ==="
161

content.text: Both rbgjl scripts confirm the core mechanism. Now let me verify the spec citations. First RBSLE and the RBS0 sections.

content.content:
2170	{rbtga_lode_vouch}::
2171	The provenance envelope carried by every
2172	{rbtga_lode}
2173	— one per Lode, batch-level — recording *capture-fidelity* rather than build-integrity.
2174	The fetched-side parallel to the made-side
2175	{rbtga_ark_vouch},
2176	and a different claim: "we faithfully captured upstream X at digest D," not "we built this securely."
2177	+
2178	It has two identical-content homes: the in-GAR `:rbi_vouch` tag (the Lode self-describing in the registry, decoded by {rbtgo_lode_augur}) and the host-side {rbf_fact_lode} (the handoff the consumption-side election reads).
2179	+
2180	Fields (schema `rbld-vouch-1`, near-term unsigned — `signature: null` — but schema-versioned):
2181	`schema`, `kind`, `lode` (the touchmark), `acquired_at` (ISO-8601 UTC), `acquired_by` (the capturing Cloud Build service account), `capture_build` (the capture's own build id), `trust_grade`, and `members[]`.
2182	Each `members[]` entry carries `name`, `origin`, `digest`, `verification`, and its assigned `tags` — so the captured identity and any Director-assigned names are themselves attested.
2183	`members[]` is the cardinality axis: length 1 for singletons (bole), N for cohorts.
2184	+
2185	Two honest trust grades, declared per Lode (Pale honesty — never over-claim):
2186	+
2187	* `verified-against-published` — bytes re-checkable against a persistent upstream (OCI content-address, or an out-of-band published checksum). The bole kind carries this grade.
2188	* `recorded-at-acquisition` — upstream offers no durable re-checkable reference (e.g. quay rotates podvm images out within days), so RB attests only the digest observed at capture. Reserved for the podvm kinds.
2189	+
2190	Future signing is reserved via cosign or the OCI referrers API once GAR maturity is confirmed.
2191	
2192	=== Foundry Fact Files
2193	
2194	[[rbf_fact_hallmark]]
2195	//axpof_fact
2196	{rbf_fact_hallmark}::
2197	Hallmark string.
2198	
2199	[[rbf_fact_build_id]]

content.text: Claims 1, 2 verified against RBSLE:123, RBS0:2178-2181, 2190. Now let me verify the grep-based negative claims (claims 3, 4) — that "referrers" is named exactly once and no referrers code exists.

content.text: Claim 4 holds with a minor refinement. The agent cited rbgjv02:269 (SLSA payload subject) and RBSAV:114,119 (DSSE subject digest) — both confirmed. There are additional `subject` hits the agent didn't enumerate (rbtdru_cupel.rs:395 is a case-statement variable named "subject"; RBSHR:307 is OIDC `principal://` subjects; RBS0:1994 is "subject to size limit" — prose). None of these is an OCI 1.1 referrers `subject` field. Media types are all standard v2/oci-index/oci-manifest with no `artifactType`/`oci-1.1` markers. The core claim — no OCI referrers/subject-field implementation — holds. Now let me verify the RBSCB and RBSHR deferral citations (claim 5, 9, 10).

content.content:
255	
256	**BuildKit provenance flag.**
257	`--provenance=true` on `docker buildx build` records builder internals — useful for deeper forensics.
258	GCB-native SLSA v1.0 provenance covers the primary attestation use case.
259	Redundant for the current threat model.
260	Revisit if build reproducibility verification becomes a priority.
261	
262	**Cosign image signing.**
263	Cryptographic signatures attached to images, verifiable by downstream consumers.
264	Two modes: keyless (Sigstore/Fulcio — requires public internet, conflicts with Egress Lockdown) and keyed (Cloud KMS — no external dependency).
265	With CB-native SLSA v1.0 Build Level 3 verified on all vessel types, cosign is redundant for the current threat model (building and consuming within own GCP project).
266	Becomes valuable if: images are distributed externally, trust verification needed outside GCP, or builder strategy expands beyond GCB (losing Google's provenance).
267	If adopted, prefer KMS-backed keyed signing (`cosign sign --key gcpkms://...`) for Egress Lockdown compatibility.
268	
269	**Binary Authorization.**
270	Deploy-time policy enforcement: only attested images can be deployed.
271	Relevant for GKE and Cloud Run.
272	Now technically achievable — CB-native SLSA Level 3 satisfies BinAuth attestation requirements.
273	Recipe Bottle deploys to Podman on workstations — no benefit until deployment targets change.
274

285	Builder step images are digest-pinned, but user-authored Dockerfiles in vessel build contexts are not validated.
286	A vessel with `FROM ubuntu:22.04` uses a mutable tag.
287	Constrains vessel authors (policy decision).
288	_Trigger_: vessel ecosystem matures beyond internal-only use.
289	_Reference_: RBSCB-CloudBuildPosture.adoc (Deferred Items: Dockerfile base image).
290	
291	**Reproducibility verification.**
292	Periodic independent rebuilds compared by digest.
293	Requires reproducible toolchains — a vessel-author discipline, not a pipeline control.
294	_Trigger_: vessel ecosystem maturity and external distribution.
295	_Reference_: RBSCB-CloudBuildPosture.adoc (Deferred Items: Reproducibility).
296	
297	**Operator federation — external-IdP identity for depot participants.**
298	Recipe Bottle grants
299	{rbtgi_depot}

content.text: RBSCB:262-267 confirms the Cosign deferral with the exact trigger language (external distribution / builder beyond GCB / redundant for current threat model). RBSHR:294 confirmed ("vessel ecosystem maturity and external distribution") — though note this is the **Reproducibility verification** entry, not a Cosign entry. The agent cited RBSHR:294 as the external-distribution trigger anchor, which is technically the reproducibility entry. Let me check whether RBSHR has a cosign-specific entry and verify the RBSHR:67-80 stale-frame claim (claim 10).

content.content:
60	Docker Compose `--wait` (v2.1.1+) blocks until all container health checks pass, which `rbob_charge` relies on to guarantee crucible readiness before returning.
61	`podman-compose` does not implement `--wait` — the command returns immediately after scheduling containers, creating a race between charge completion and the active-check assertion in theurge.
62	Without `--wait`, Podman integration will need an alternative readiness gate (polling loop, explicit health-check query, or `podman wait --condition=healthy`).
63	_Trigger_: Podman first-class deployment (above).
64	_Reference_: containers/podman-compose#710 (June 2023, open);
65	containers/podman-compose#1329 (October 2025, open).
66	
67	**Revised enshrine — universal capture verb under Lode recipe noun.**
68	Today's narrow `enshrine` mirrors only upstream OCI base images.
69	Three additional capture needs share the same gesture (pull from upstream, SLSA-wrap, store in GAR): build-time tool images (today's `mirror`/`reliquary` machinery), the WSL substrate distro that hosts the Windows workload, and podman VM disk images.
70	The revision generalizes `enshrine` into a universal verb operating on a single recipe noun — **Lode**, parallel to {rbtga_vessel} on the built side — with a kind enum that gates pipeline behavior: `base`, `tool`, `reliquary`, `wsl`, `podvm-wsl`, `podvm-native`.
71	Today's `reliquary` demotes from top-level noun to a kind enum slot — the date-cohort semantic survives, the cult-vocabulary weight drops.
72	Today's `mirror` verb retires entirely;
73	absorbed into enshrine on the `tool` and `reliquary` kinds.
74	Lode .env carries author intent only — no resolved FQIN field — mirroring RBRV's ORIGIN/ANCHOR pattern (kindle code computes upstream coordinates from kind primitives at module-startup).
75	The `wsl` kind delivers a project-controlled WSL rootfs tar that replaces the Microsoft-Store-appx-mutable distribution today's caparison-w depends on;
76	the `podvm-*` kinds collapse `rbv_PodmanVM.sh`'s existing GHCR mirror into GAR under the unified verb.
77	_Trigger_: when Windows host workload requires a project-controlled WSL distro, when podman VM supply chain needs project-owned bytes in GAR, or when the `mirror`/`reliquary` machinery accumulates enough friction to motivate consolidation.
78	_Reference_: RBSAE-ark_enshrine.adoc (current narrow-scope spec);
79	RBSRV-RegimeVessel.adoc (ORIGIN/ANCHOR precedent, kind-enum-gated variable groups pattern);
80	`Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/FUTURE/rbv_PodmanVM.sh` (existing podman-vm fan-out machinery).
81	
82	**Hairpin peer access between crucibles.**
83	Bottled containers cannot currently reach each other's published entry ports.
84	Each bottle is isolated behind its own sentry on a separate enclave network.

input.description: Grep README for captures, CLAUDE.md delegation, cosign, roadmap items

content.content: 265:Knowing where an image came from — and proving it — is the domain of software supply-chain security. The stabilized toolchain pairs build provenance ([SLSA](https://slsa.dev)) with cryptographic signing ([Sigstore](https://www.sigstore.dev)/cosign), a [software bill of materials](#SBOM) ([Syft](https://github.com/anchore/syft)), vulnerability scanning, and deploy-time admission control ([Kyverno](https://kyverno.io), OPA Gatekeeper) that rejects unsigned or unattested images. 340:[Binding](#Bind) captures an external image at a specific digest into the [Depot's](#Depot) registry. 431:[Scrying](#Scry) captures packets on the [Crucible's](#Crucible) network interfaces — for verifying that blocked traffic is actually blocked, diagnosing connectivity issues, or watching the [Sentry's](#Sentry) filtering in action. 538:- **[Crucible Conduit for Cloud Services](#CrucibleConduit)** - Encrypted tunnel from the [Sentry](#Sentry) to a VPC hosting PrivateLink endpoints, enabling [Bottles](#Bottle) to reach cloud AI services (AWS Bedrock, Vertex AI, Azure OpenAI) without exposing floating cloud IP ranges in the CIDR allowlist. 545:Orthogonal to but paired with the [Crucible Conduit](#CrucibleConduit): the conduit gives the [Bottle](#Bottle) network reach to a cloud service while the [Sentry](#Sentry) holds the tunnel key, and this holds the service secret inside the [Bottle](#Bottle). 551:- **[VPC Service Controls](#VpcServiceControls)** - Google Cloud security perimeters that prevent data from being copied out of a project even if an attacker holds valid credentials. 553:If a VPC is stood up for the [Crucible Conduit](#CrucibleConduit) architecture, the VPC-SC perimeter should serve both Cloud Build [egress lockdown](#BuildIsolation) and [Bottle](#Bottle) conduit consumers. 555:- **[Cosign Container Signing](#CosignSigning)** - Cryptographic image signatures independent of registry trust. 556:Deferred alongside VPC Service Controls until external distribution triggers the need. 576:| `├── CLAUDE.md` | [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code) command reference, glossary, conventions |

input.description: Grep README for glossary verbs Bind/Reliquary/Enshrine/Graft

content.content: 76:Three [Vessel](#Vessel) modes determine how images enter the [Depot](#Depot): [Conjure](#Conjure) ([egress-locked](#BuildIsolation) build from source with [SLSA provenance](#Provenance)), [Bind](#Bind) (digest-pinned upstream mirror), and [Graft](#Graft) (local push). 92:[Airgap](#Airgap) builds draw all dependencies from [Enshrined](#Enshrine) images in the [Depot's](#Depot) registry — fully self-contained, independent of upstream availability. 93:Requires [Enshrining](#Enshrine) base images before the first build. 128:A specification for a container image — built from source ([Conjure](#Conjure)), mirrored from upstream ([Bind](#Bind)), or pushed from local ([Graft](#Graft)). 162:Before the first build can run, the [Depot](#Depot) needs its supply-chain infrastructure in place: upstream base images must be [Enshrined](#Enshrine) into the registry, and a [Reliquary](#Reliquary) of builder tool images must be inscribed. 171:The [Director](#Director) [Ordains](#Ordain) [Hallmarks](#Hallmark) for each [Vessel](#Vessel) — [Conjuring](#Conjure) from source, [Binding](#Bind) from upstream, or [Grafting](#Graft) from local builds. 184:- Upstream base images [Enshrined](#Enshrine) into the [Depot's](#Depot) registry, so builds do not depend on third-party registry availability at build time 252:The [pluml](#pluml) [Nameplate](#Nameplate) pairs the [Sentry](#Sentry) with a [Bind](#Bind)-mode PlantUML [Bottle](#Bottle) — an upstream image pinned by digest — under a no-egress allowlist: the renderer needs no internet, so the [Crucible](#Crucible) grants it none. It exercises the [Bind](#Bind) supply-chain path and the most restrictive network posture. 322:**[Enshrine](#Enshrine)** — Mirror upstream base images into your [Depot's](#Depot) registry. 323:[Enshrining](#Enshrine) ensures the build pipeline has a fixed, self-contained supply chain — builds draw from project-owned copies rather than depending on third-party registry availability at build time. 325:**[Reliquary](#Reliquary)** — Co-versioned set of builder tool images (skopeo, docker, gcloud, syft) inscribed from upstream into the [Depot's](#Depot) registry. 326:Cloud Build jobs use [Reliquary](#Reliquary) images as step containers, ensuring builds run with known, project-owned toolchains rather than pulling tools from upstream at build time. 327:The [Director](#Director) inscribes a [Reliquary](#Reliquary) before any [Ordain](#Ordain) or [Enshrine](#Enshrine) operation can run. 332:[Ordaining](#Ordain) is mode-aware: it [Conjures](#Conjure), [Binds](#Bind), or [Grafts](#Graft) depending on the [Vessel's](#Vessel) configuration. 339:**[Bind](#Bind)** — Mirror an upstream image pinned by digest. 340:[Binding](#Bind) captures an external image at a specific digest into the [Depot's](#Depot) registry. 343:**[Graft](#Graft)** — Push a locally-built image to the [Depot](#Depot) registry. 344:[Grafting](#Graft) uploads a local image to GAR via docker push — no Cloud Build for the image itself, though [About](#About) and [Vouch](#Vouch) metadata still run in Cloud Build. 345:This is the lowest-trust mode (GRAFTED verdict). 366:The [Vouch](#Vouch) verdict is mode-aware: [Conjure](#Conjure) builds receive full [SLSA provenance](#Provenance) verification, [Bind](#Bind) builds receive digest-pin verification, and [Graft](#Graft) builds receive a GRAFTED verdict with no [provenance](#Provenance) chain. 461:| [**Bind**](#Bind) | Digest-pin comparison — image in GAR matches pinned upstream reference | Digest-pin match | 462:| [**Graft**](#Graft) | Locally built and pushed — no cloud build involvement | GRAFTED (explicit no-provenance marker) | 493:[Airgap](#Airgap) does not mean "nothing external." It means all external content enters through a single auditable gate — the [Enshrine](#Enshrine) ceremony — rather than ad-hoc network fetches during build. 494:The attack surface collapses from "any URL a Dockerfile mentions" to "the specific digests the [Director](#Director) [Enshrined](#Enshrine)." 495:Builder tool images enter through a parallel gate: the [Reliquary](#Reliquary), inscribed once and pinned by digest for all subsequent builds. 500:Base images are vetted separately: digest-pinned at [Enshrine](#Enshrine) time, inspectable via [SBOM](#SBOM), and stored as project-owned copies in the [Depot's](#Depot) registry. 605:| ` ├── rbev-sentry-deb-airgap/` | [Conjure](#Conjure) — [Sentry](#Sentry) (airgapped, enshrined bases) | 623:| ` ├── rbev-bottle-plantuml/` | [Bind](#Bind) — upstream image pinned by digest | 624:| ` │ └── rbrv.env` | [RBRV](#RBRV) — [Bind](#Bind) mode, digest reference | 652:**[RBRV](#RBRV)** — [Vessel](#Vessel) configuration specifying [Bind](#Bind), [Conjure](#Conjure), or [Graft](#Graft) mode for creating [Hallmarks](#Hallmark).

content.text:
All README claims verified. Let me confirm the specific glossary line numbers the agent cited and check the "captures" prose at 340/431 and Reliquary at 325-327.

The grep confirms:
- README:340 — Bind glossary "captures an external image at a specific digest" (agent cited 339-340 ✓; "captures" prose at 340 ✓)
- README:431 — Scry "captures packets" (the other generic "captures" hit ✓)
- README:325-327 — Reliquary glossary ✓
- README:322-323 — Enshrine glossary (agent cited "Enshrine referenced at 162/327"; the glossary *definition* is actually at 322 — minor, both 162 and 327 do reference Enshrine ✓)
- README:576 — CLAUDE.md delegation "command reference, glossary, conventions" ✓
- README:538/551/555-556 — Roadmap appendix: Crucible Conduit, VPC-SC, Cosign ✓
- README:556 — Cosign "Deferred alongside VPC Service Controls until external distribution triggers the need" ✓

Let me verify the Roadmap appendix boundary (534-566) and confirm Podman appears in it, and double-check the RBSLE line 123 cite was correct (it was — verified above). Let me also confirm there's no Lode/per-kind-verb in the Roadmap appendix range.

content.content: 534 ## Appendix: Roadmap 535 536 The following features are not yet implemented but are under consideration: 537 538 - **[Crucible Conduit for Cloud Services](#CrucibleConduit)** - Encrypted tunnel from the [Sentry](#Sentry) to a VPC hosting PrivateLink endpoints, enabling [Bottles](#Bottle) to reach cloud AI services (AWS Bedrock, Vertex AI, Azure OpenAI) without exposing floating cloud IP ranges in the CIDR allowlist. 539 WireGuard terminated at the [Sentry](#Sentry) replaces per-service IP tracking with a single stable VPC CIDR. 540 Near-term, allowlist-only [Nameplates](#Nameplate) targeting specific service CIDRs and domains work today with existing [Sentry](#Sentry) machinery. 541 The tunnel adds defense-in-depth for PrivateLink-capable services; SaaS endpoints without PrivateLink (GitHub.com, GitLab.com) remain served by CIDR/domain allowlisting. 542 543 - **[Bottle Credential Custody](#BottleCredentialCustody)** - Move the service secrets a [Bottle](#Bottle) workload uses (cloud API keys, IAM keys, SSH keys) off the operator's workstation and into the [Bottle](#Bottle) itself, injected at [Charge](#Charge) time via [Nameplate](#Nameplate) [Regime](#Regime) configuration — never baked into the image. 544 The workstation [Charges](#Charge) the [Crucible](#Crucible) but never holds the secret; its only credential becomes permission to charge, which blocks credential theft from a compromised workstation. 545 Orthogonal to but paired with the [Crucible Conduit](#CrucibleConduit): the conduit gives the [Bottle](#Bottle) network reach to a cloud service while the [Sentry](#Sentry) holds the tunnel key, and this holds the service secret inside the [Bottle](#Bottle). 546 547 - **[Operator Federation](#OperatorFederation)** - The path to corporate-acceptable identity — admit operators through an external identity provider (OIDC/SAML) instead of long-lived service-account keys on disk, with identity proved by a fresh sign-in and capabilities granted to a federated principal, no secret at rest. 548 This tier **requires a GCP organization**, which is the dividing line from today's keyfile model (which needs none): a qualifying organization is free via Google Cloud Identity once you verify ownership of a **DNS domain**, so the real prerequisite is controlling a domain, not paying Google. 549 Revocation becomes central at the identity provider rather than a manual, per-key [Divest](#Divest). 550 551 - **[VPC Service Controls](#VpcServiceControls)** - Google Cloud security perimeters that prevent data from being copied out of a project even if an attacker holds valid credentials. 552 [Recipe Bottle's](#RecipeBottle) Cloud Build architecture uses private pools, which are the prerequisite for VPC enforcement; enabling the controls themselves is deferred until organizational policy or external distribution requires them. 553 If a VPC is stood up for the [Crucible Conduit](#CrucibleConduit) architecture, the VPC-SC perimeter should serve both Cloud Build [egress lockdown](#BuildIsolation) and [Bottle](#Bottle) conduit consumers. 554 555 - **[Cosign Container Signing](#CosignSigning)** - Cryptographic image signatures independent of registry trust. 556 Deferred alongside VPC Service Controls until external distribution triggers the need. 557 558 - **[CDN-Aware IP Gating](#CdnAwareIpGating)** - When allowed domains are CDN-hosted (e.g. Cloudflare), the [Sentry's](#Sentry) CIDR allowlist becomes coarse: DNS-level gating remains precise, but IP-level gating is porous across shared CDN address ranges. 559 A tighter mechanism is recognized but not yet designed. 560 561 - **[Podman Support](#PodmanSupport)** - The spec accommodates Podman as an alternative container runtime, but support is deferred. 562 On macOS, both Docker and Podman run Linux containers inside a hidden Linux VM — there is no native container runtime on Darwin. 563 Podman support would require managing that VM's lifecycle within the customer's [Depot](#Depot), adding infrastructure complexity with no architectural advantage over Docker Desktop. 564 565 - **[Crucible-to-Crucible Networking](#CrucibleToCrucible)** - Under the current [Sentry](#Sentry) model, [Bottles](#Bottle) have no direct network path to each other; any inter-[Bottle](#Bottle) communication would route through their respective [Sentries](#Sentry). 566 The plumbing is feasible but not implemented, pending a concrete use case.

content.text:
Claim 9 fully verified. Roadmap appendix (534-566) lists Crucible Conduit, Bottle Credential Custody, Operator Federation, VPC-SC, Cosign, CDN-Aware IP Gating, Podman, Crucible-to-Crucible — and does NOT enumerate Lode kinds or capture verbs. Cosign at 555-556 with external-distribution trigger ✓.

I have all the evidence I need. Let me do a final cross-check on claim 6 (the Pale framing — it's the one marked "medium" confidence and is interpretive). The RBS0:2190 language "once GAR maturity is confirmed" is confirmed verbatim. The Pale doctrine mapping is the agent's own interpretation layered on CMK ROE — defensible but interpretive, which the agent honestly flagged as medium.

Let me also double-check one thing the agent asserts in the recommendation that's load-bearing for parallelism: that a new kind "never touches provenance attachment" because the spine/body split means a new kind authors only a recipe+blob. Let me verify the spine contract claim quickly.

content.content:
19:# Recipe Bottle Lode - capture-assembly spine (guard-free cluster, sourced by
21:# kind. Takes a recipe (ordered, pre-resolved step rows) plus an opaque
22:# substitutions blob, composes the Build resource, submits, polls, and decodes a
23:# step's buildStepOutputs slot. Owns NO kind knowledge: the recipe and the
24:# substitutions are data; the only kind-flavored build knob (the poll ceiling) is
38:# Internal: dispatch-time substitution-coverage check. A capture step reads
39:# automapped _RBGL_* substitution registers; a reference to a key the body's
40:# substitutions blob never defines expands to empty inside the build and corrupts
44:# its substitution requires — no separate declaration to drift from the code — so
46:# at the first reference absent from the blob's keys, logging the offending
50:# Coverage is flat by design (RBSCJ "Substitution-coverage check"): substitutions
56:  local -r z_keys_file="${1:?Substitution keys file required}"
63:  # keys (a blob declaring no registers) is legitimate — no test -n.
65:  local -r z_keys_blob=$'\n'"${z_keys}"$'\n'
89:      case "${z_keys_blob}" in
91:        *) buc_log_args "Uncovered substitution register: ${z_ref}"; return 1 ;;
100:# Internal: compose, submit, and poll a Lode capture Cloud Build from a recipe
101:# plus an opaque substitutions blob.
103:# A recipe row is a |-delimited 4-tuple: script_path|builder_image|id|entrypoint
111:# The substitutions file holds a JSON object the spine slots verbatim into the
112:# Build envelope's `substitutions` field; the spine reads no key from it. The
113:# envelope shape (serviceAccount, options.automapSubstitutions, options.logging,
117:# Args: token label poll_ceiling subs_file temp_prefix recipe_row...
124:  local -r z_subs_file="${1:?Substitutions file required}"; shift
126:  test "$#" -ge 1 || buc_die "zrbld_spine_dispatch: recipe requires at least one step row"

content.text:
The spine confirms it owns NO kind knowledge — a new kind authors a recipe (step rows) plus a substitutions blob. The provenance attachment (`:rbi_vouch` push) is handled by the shared rbgjl02 step which is recipe-level, not kind-specific. This supports the recommendation's claim that a new vertical "never touches provenance attachment." The "envelope" hits here are the Cloud Build envelope (the Build resource), not the vouch envelope — distinct usage, but the kind authoring still doesn't touch the vouch mechanism.

I have thoroughly verified all load-bearing claims. Let me compile the verdict.

verdicts.claim: Lode provenance rides as a reserved plain GAR tag :rbi_vouch (FROM-scratch manifest carrying vouch.json) pushed into the SAME package as the captured base manifest; no OCI referrers/subject-linking, just another tag pointed-to by the touchmark.
verdicts.note: Verified. rbgjl02-assemble-push-vouch.sh:43-44 (`echo FROM scratch` + `COPY vouch.json /`), pushed to `.../<STAMP>:${_RBGL_TAG_VOUCH}` at line 37/47. Header comment lines 10-13 + 21: 'Each envelope rides into the SAME package as the base manifest, under the :rbi_vouch tag — a distinct manifest'. rbgjl01:21 'The :rbi_vouch tag is a separate manifest pushed by step 02.' RBSLE:123 confirms verbatim: 'build a FROM scratch image copying vouch.json and push it to the Lode package under the :rbi_vouch tag — a distinct manifest sharing the one package.' No subject-field/referrers used.

verdicts.claim: Vouch envelope authored entirely in step 01 as hand-built JSON (no jq), staged to /workspace, lands in two identical-content homes (in-GAR :rbi_vouch tag + host-side rbf_fact_lode); schema-versioned (rbld-vouch-1) and explicitly unsigned (signature:null).
verdicts.note: Verified. rbgjl01:121-139 assembles ENVELOPE as string concatenation, no jq (comment line 118 'No jq dependency'); signature:null at line 129; staged at 139 (`/workspace/lode_${STAMP}_vouch.json`); same envelope written to host channel via RESULT at 148/158. RBS0:2178 'two identical-content homes: the in-GAR :rbi_vouch tag ... and the host-side {rbf_fact_lode}'. RBS0:2180 'schema rbld-vouch-1, near-term unsigned — signature: null — but schema-versioned'. Schema value comes from substitution _RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA (line 122), not a hardcoded literal, but the spec pins it to rbld-vouch-1.

verdicts.claim: OCI referrers API named in exactly ONE place across spec/code corpus, as a reserved FUTURE option co-equal with cosign, gated 'once GAR maturity is confirmed'; no design/pace/contract depends on it.
verdicts.note: Verified. `grep -rni referrer Tools/rbk/` returns exactly one hit: RBS0:2190 'Future signing is reserved via cosign or the OCI referrers API once GAR maturity is confirmed.' Sole occurrence confirmed.

verdicts.claim: No OCI referrers/subject-field implementation anywhere; the only `subject` hits are SLSA DSSE subject digests; media-type lists are standard v2/oci-index/oci-manifest; the :rbi_vouch tag-in-same-package design sidesteps referrers.
verdicts.note: Substantively verified with a small completeness caveat. Cited subject hits confirmed: rbgjv02-verify-provenance.py:269 (SLSA payload subject) and RBSAV:114,119 (DSSE subject digest). Additional `subject` hits exist that the agent did NOT enumerate: rbtdru_cupel.rs:395 (a case-statement variable literally named 'subject'), RBSHR:307 (OIDC `principal://` subjects), RBS0:1994 ('subject to ~4KB limit' prose). None is an OCI 1.1 referrers `subject` field. No `artifactType`/`oci-1.1` markers anywhere; all media-type lists are standard docker-v2/oci-index/oci-manifest. Core claim (no referrers impl) holds.

verdicts.claim: Referrers/cosign deferral anchored to a concrete external trigger documented in RBSCB Deferred Items and RBSHR roadmap: external distribution / builder beyond GCB; until then SLSA L3 + tag-borne vouch cover the threat model, so signing is redundant.
verdicts.note: Substance verified; ONE citation line-number is off. RBSCB:262-267 confirmed verbatim (cosign 'redundant for the current threat model (building and consuming within own GCP project)'; 'Becomes valuable if: images are distributed externally ... or builder strategy expands beyond GCB'). The cosign-specific trigger in RBSHR is at line 270-274 ('Cosign image signing ... _Trigger_: external image distribution ... or builder strategy expanding beyond Google Cloud Build'), NOT line 294. RBSHR:294 is the *Reproducibility verification* entry's trigger ('vessel ecosystem maturity and external distribution'). The agent cited RBSHR:294 for the cosign anchor — correct anchor is RBSHR:274. Substance unaffected.

verdicts.claim: GAR referrers support is itself a Pale (foreign-vendor, verdict-less) question — 'once GAR maturity is confirmed' frames it as waiting on Google, not RB engineering; verifying it is unanchored research.
verdicts.note: RBS0:2190 language 'once GAR maturity is confirmed' verified verbatim. The Pale mapping (CMK ROE 'Can I edit the source? No -> Pale') is the agent's own interpretation layered onto the spec text — defensible (GAR maturity is uneditable vendor behavior), and the agent honestly graded it medium confidence. Interpretive, not a hard factual claim; holds as a reasonable reading.

verdicts.claim: Public README glossary is concept/made-side only (Enshrine, Reliquary, Bind, Graft) with ZERO Lode/touchmark/per-kind-verb mentions — Lode subsystem entirely absent from public README.
verdicts.note: Verified. `grep -ni 'lode|touchmark|ensconce|fetter|conclave|underpin|immure|augur|divine|banish' README.md` returns zero hits. Glossary entries confirmed: Enshrine README:322-323, Reliquary 325-327, Bind 339-340, Graft 343-345. The two generic 'captures' prose hits (Bind 340, Scry 431) confirmed — neither is the Lode subsystem. Lode subsystem fully absent from README.

verdicts.claim: README delegates glossary/command-reference to CLAUDE.md, so the public README is a curated concept surface; per-kind verbs live in CLAUDE.md acronym maps + veiled RBSL* specs.
verdicts.note: Verified. README:576 '| `├── CLAUDE.md` | Claude Code command reference, glossary, conventions |'. Per-kind Lode verbs (rbw-lE/ld/lB) documented in claude-rbk-tabtarget-context.md Lode section; RBSLE/RBSLA specs are veiled. README is a concept surface, not exhaustive verb catalog.

verdicts.claim: README Roadmap appendix lists future features (Crucible Conduit, Cosign, VPC-SC, Podman, etc.) but does NOT enumerate Lode kinds or capture verbs; Cosign appears once as generic deferred item with the same external-distribution trigger.
verdicts.note: Verified by reading README:534-566. Appendix enumerates Crucible Conduit, Bottle Credential Custody, Operator Federation, VPC-SC, Cosign, CDN-Aware IP Gating, Podman, Crucible-to-Crucible. No Lode kinds / capture verbs present. Cosign at 555-556: 'Deferred alongside VPC Service Controls until external distribution triggers the need.' Confirmed.

verdicts.claim: RBSHR still describes capture work under the OLD frame ('Revised enshrine — universal capture verb under Lode recipe noun', kind enum base/tool/reliquary/wsl/podvm, 'mirror verb retires') with NO per-kind verb names; stale relative to landed bole vertical but internal/veiled.
verdicts.note: Verified strongly. RBSHR:67-80 reads 'Revised enshrine — universal capture verb under Lode recipe noun', kind enum 'base, tool, reliquary, wsl, podvm-wsl, podvm-native' (line 70), 'Today's mirror verb retires entirely' (line 72). `grep` of RBSHR for ensconce/fetter/conclave/underpin/immure returns zero — no per-kind verb names present. Stale frame confirmed; file is under vov_veiled/ (internal).

input.corrections:
The recommendation STANDS — both halves (defer referrers; single concept-level public-docs pace riding the enshrine/inscribe cutover, no per-vertical README pace) are well-grounded in the files I re-read. Two citation slips and one completeness gap to flag, none of which change the recommendation:

1. CITATION SLIP (claim 5 / recommendation part a, point 3): The agent cites "RBSHR:294" as the cosign/external-distribution deferral anchor. RBSHR:294 is actually the *Reproducibility verification* entry's trigger. The correct cosign anchor is RBSHR:270-274 (the explicit 'Cosign image signing' entry, _Trigger_ line 274: 'external image distribution ... or builder strategy expanding beyond Google Cloud Build'). The substance is unaffected — RBSHR does document the cosign/external-distribution trigger, just at 270-274 not 294 — and RBSCB:262-267 (the agent's other cite for this point) is correct verbatim.

2. COMPLETENESS GAP (claim 4): The agent's grep narrative implies the only `subject` hits are the two SLSA/DSSE ones. There are three more `subject` occurrences in Tools/rbk (rbtdru_cupel.rs:395 a case-statement var named 'subject'; RBSHR:307 OIDC principal subjects; RBS0:1994 'subject to ... limit' prose). None is an OCI 1.1 referrers `subject` field, so the load-bearing conclusion (no referrers implementation) is correct — but the enumeration was not exhaustive.

3. MINOR (claim 7): Agent says 'Enshrine referenced at 162/327'; the actual Enshrine *glossary definition* is at README:322-323 (162 and 327 are references to it). Trivial, does not affect anything.

Two confirmations worth recording because they directly underwrite the recommendation's parallelism argument:
- The vouch SCHEMA value in rbgjl01 comes from the _RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA substitution (line 122), not a hardcoded 'rbld-vouch-1' literal in the step; the spec (RBS0:2180) is what pins it to rbld-vouch-1. The 'schema-versioned with a null signature slot = clean upgrade seam' claim holds.
- I independently confirmed the spine/body split underpinning 'a new kind never touches provenance attachment': rblds_Spine.sh:19-24 states the spine 'Owns NO kind knowledge' — a kind authors a recipe (step rows) + an opaque substitutions blob, and the :rbi_vouch push lives in the shared recipe-level rbgjl02 step. So the recommendation's file-disjointness / no-collision-on-provenance argument is sound.

content.content: Structured output provided successfully

content.text: I have completed the independent verification. All ten load-bearing claims hold against the actual files. The recommendation stands, with two minor citation slips and one completeness gap recorded in corrections — none of which change the conclusion.
